summary: The commenter, a patient and family caregiver, expresses concern that removing certification requirements will reduce data availability and transparency, especially around AI use and privacy/security features. They support FHIR's future potential but urge caution and recommend maintaining or transitioning certification requirements to ensure patient access and trust.
key_arguments: Patients and caregivers rarely use information blocking threats, so data should be easily available by default.
Removing certification will lead vendors and providers to drop features, harming interoperability and data exchange.
AI transparency is critical for patient trust and should be part of the accessible health record.
Privacy and security features are essential and should not be removed as breaches are too late to address harm.
recommendations: Replace removed certification requirements with attestation or delay removal until FHIR replacements are widely adopted.
Maintain AI transparency requirements in EHRs to allow patients to understand AI use.
Retain privacy and security certification criteria to ensure protections and audit capabilities remain.
Advance FHIR-based patient functionalities such as structured writes, appointment scheduling, and electronic requests.
notable_quote: Data should just be easily available, and the certification program... supports that.
